description: Canonical lineup correction manifest seeded from live runtime override artifacts.

corrections.evidence_summary: Q4 Kings raw PBP shows repeated Lionel Simmons 1489 events while the active lineup omitted him; family register flagged bad_v6_boundary_row; override matches the v5 Kings row and replaces the bad v6 row that used 782 instead of 1489
corrections.notes: 1997 bad-v6 cluster | matches v5 exact team set

corrections.evidence_summary: Q3 Kings raw issue rows repeatedly credit Corliss Williamson 722 while the bad v6 row carries Lionel Simmons 1489; the override matches the exact v4 and v5 Kings team row, not v6.
corrections.notes: 1997 bad-v6 cluster | matches v4 and v5 exact team set

corrections.evidence_summary: Q2 Hawks raw issue rows credit Henry James 1080 early in the quarter while the v6 ATL row omitted him for 895; family register says ATL P2 v6 row is wrong and override matches the v5 ATL row
corrections.notes: 1997 bad-v6 cluster | matches v5 exact team set

corrections.evidence_summary: Q2 Warriors raw issue rows show Joe Smith 693 and Andrew DeClercq 692 events at 11:23 and 10:57 while the v6 row kept 923 and 899 active; override matches the v5 Warriors row
corrections.notes: 1997 bad-v6 cluster | matches v5 exact team set

corrections.evidence_summary: Q2 Spurs raw issue rows show Cory Alexander 724 assist credit at 10:47 while the v6 row kept Dominique Wilkins 1122 active; override matches the v5 Spurs row
corrections.notes: 1997 bad-v6 cluster | matches v5 exact team set

corrections.evidence_summary: Q2 Warriors v6 row used 923 instead of Andrew DeClercq 692 and the raw issue rows immediately credit DeClercq early in the quarter; local repair used a team-level raw-PBP reconstruction rather than trusting either full v5 or full v6 row
corrections.notes: 1997 bad-v6 cluster | neither v5 nor v6 full game-period row was trusted wholesale

corrections.evidence_summary: Q2 Jazz raw issue rows show 11:41 SUB Keefe FOR Ostertag and 10:10 Keefe assist which implies Ostertag 731 starts the period and Keefe 228 enters later; local repair corrected the Jazz team row instead of taking the v6 row with 935 or the full v5 row blindly
corrections.notes: 1997 bad-v6 cluster | targeted team-level repair

Green P4 E511 but leaves Dirk P2 E211 off-court, and it creates two extra sub-0.1-minute mismatches for Bradley and Green while pushing the game from 1 to 3 minute-mismatch rows.

corrections.evidence_summary: Raw pbpv3 credits Emmanuel Mudiay's P3 1:31 field goal before the same-clock Burke-for-Mudiay and Thomas-for-Beasley substitutions, while the issue row currently carries the settled post-sub lineup.
corrections.notes: Accepted on 2026-03-22 after one-game scratch validation cleared the lone `P3 E482` Mudiay event-on-court blocker with no minute mismatches or counting-stat regressions. The validated pre-sub Knicks five is `Mudiay / Hardaway Jr. / Beasley / O'Quinn / Ntilikina`, and the game now falls to a tiny two-row boundary-difference PM tail instead of an open lineup blocker.

corrections.evidence_summary: Raw pbpv3 and playbyplayv2 credit Keith Van Horn with a 2:23 Q2 shooting foul before the same-clock Van Horn-for-Aaron Williams substitution, while full_pbp_new already has Van Horn on the 2:23 possession lineup.
corrections.notes: Rejected on 2026-03-22 after scratch validation traded the original `P2 E227` Van Horn foul blocker for `P2 E229` (`SUB: Van Horn FOR Williams`) and left the 0.25-minute Van Horn tail unchanged, while also introducing two small plus-minus mismatches for Van Horn and Aaron Williams.

corrections.evidence_summary: Raw pbpv3 shows Clarke and Jackson Jr. entering at 6:42 before the 6:40 Jackson Jr. rebound; full_pbp_new and cross-source minutes support the post-sub Memphis lineup at the rebound event.
corrections.notes: Rejected on 2026-03-22 after scratch validation kept one event-on-court issue alive at `P2 E239` (`SUB: Jones FOR Morant`) and introduced six small minute mismatches, even though counting-stat boxscore audit stayed clean.

corrections.evidence_summary: Raw pbpv3 shows Harrell entering for Zubac at 0:03.4 before the 0:01.4 Harrell rebound; full_pbp_new and cross-source minutes support the post-sub Clippers lineup at the rebound event.
corrections.notes: Rejected on 2026-03-22 after scratch validation cleared the rebound row but left `P2 E348` (`SUB: Harrell FOR Zubac`) as a live event-on-court blocker and introduced two small minute mismatches, even though counting-stat boxscore audit stayed clean.

corrections.evidence_summary: Raw pbpv3 shows Tolliver and Jones entering for Memphis at 2:26 before the 2:23 Tolliver rebound; full_pbp_new and cross-source minutes support the post-sub Memphis lineup at the rebound event.
corrections.notes: Rejected on 2026-03-22 after scratch validation cleared the rebound row but left `P2 E307` (`SUB: Tolliver FOR Anderson`) as a live event-on-court blocker and introduced four small minute mismatches, even though counting-stat boxscore audit stayed clean.

corrections.evidence_summary: Raw pbpv3 shows Harrell entering for Zubac at 0:03.4 before the 0:01.4 Harrell rebound; full_pbp_new and cross-source minutes support the post-sub Clippers lineup across the rest of the same-clock cluster.
corrections.notes: Rejected on 2026-03-22 after widening the correction to the full 0:03.4-0:01.4 same-clock cluster still left `P2 E348` (`SUB: Harrell FOR Zubac`) as a live event-on-court blocker and preserved the same two small minute mismatches, even though counting-stat boxscore audit stayed clean.

corrections.evidence_summary: Raw pbpv3 shows Tolliver and Jones entering for Memphis at 2:26 before the 2:23 Tolliver rebound; full_pbp_new and cross-source minutes support the post-sub Memphis lineup across the rest of the same-clock cluster.
corrections.notes: Rejected on 2026-03-22 after widening the correction to the full 2:26-2:23 same-clock cluster still left `P2 E312` (`Tolliver REBOUND`) as a live event-on-court blocker and produced no minute-mismatch improvement at all, even though counting-stat boxscore audit stayed clean.

corrections.evidence_summary: Raw pbpv3 and playbyplayv2 place the Noel-for-Adams substitution before Noel's 7:42 Q3 rebound, while full_pbp_new flips OKC from Adams on the 7:49 possession to Noel on the 7:42 possession.
corrections.notes: Rejected on 2026-03-22 after one-game scratch validation moved the blocker from `P3 E398` (`Noel REBOUND`) to `P3 E395` (`SUB: Noel FOR Adams`) and introduced two small minute mismatches (Nerlens Noel / Steven Adams at 0.05 minutes each) plus two small plus-minus mismatches (Kentavious Caldwell-Pope / Alex Caruso), even though counting-stat boxscore audit stayed clean.

corrections.evidence_summary: Raw playbyplayv2 shows `SUB: Harkless FOR Williams` at 4:42 before the 4:38 Harkless rebound, while full_pbp_new flips the Clippers from Lou Williams on the 4:46 possession to Maurice Harkless on the 4:38 possession.
corrections.notes: Rejected on 2026-03-22 after one-game scratch validation moved the blocker from `P2 E258` (`Harkless REBOUND`) to `P2 E255` (`SUB: Harkless FOR Williams`) and introduced two small minute mismatches (Lou Williams / Maurice Harkless at 0.0667 minutes each) plus two small plus-minus mismatches (Patrick Beverley / Patrick Patterson), even though counting-stat boxscore audit stayed clean.

residual_annotations.notes: Dirk Nowitzki P2 E211 miss is explicitly documented as source-limited under current Block A policy. Apply to the Dirk-linked event row and the downstream 0.100 minute residual, but not to the separate A.C. Green P4 E511 tradeoff row.

Tony Battie is credited with P3 E435 offensive goaltending turnover and P3 E441 block while absent from both current and previous Boston lineups. Treat both event rows and the associated 0.083333 minute drift as source-limited upstream credit error.

Vinny Del Negro is credited with P5 E544 foul while absent from both current and previous Milwaukee OT lineups. Treat the event row and the linked 0.200000 minute residual as source-limited upstream credit error.
P3 E369 defensive-3-second team foul is credited to Doug West even though he is off both current and previous Memphis lineups. Keep runtime lineup and annotate the upstream team-foul miscredit only.
P2 E245 miss is credited to Michael Curry even though he is off both current and previous Detroit lineups. Keep runtime lineup and annotate the upstream shot-credit error only.

Kendall Gill has no event-on-court rows in the live Block A rerun, and the current output (36.7) stays close to tpdev_pbp (36.55) while official (32.72) and BBR (31.37) diverge by 3.98 to 5.33 minutes. Treat this as a source-limited upstream minute conflict rather than a local lineup bug.

Damon Jones has no event-on-court rows in the live Block A rerun, and the current output (11.3) stays close to tpdev_pbp (11.68) while official and BBR both report 16.63. Treat this as a source-limited upstream minute conflict rather than a local lineup bug.

Detlef Schrempf is credited with P1/P2 events despite the NBA PBP never recording his P2 entry, and the live output minute total (19.745) stays effectively aligned with tpdev_pbp (19.733) while official/BBR both sit at 20.833. The rejected starter episode leaves this as a documented upstream chronology/substitution gap, not a remaining local correction.

Jim McIlvaine is credited with P2 rebound/block events even though the NBA PBP never records his P2 entry. The live output minute total (17.8) stays close to tpdev_pbp (17.833) and only 0.117 off official/BBR, so keep the runtime lineup and treat the residual as an upstream missing-substitution source gap.

Jim Jackson reappears for a P2 shot without a clean NBA PBP re-entry event. The live output minute total (30.883) stays closer to tpdev_pbp (31.0) than to official/BBR (31.15), and the starter/window attempts were rejected, so this remaining row is best treated as a source-limited upstream chronology conflict.

Theo Ratliff is credited with a P2 1:11 foul after BBR shows him subbed out at 1:25. The live output minute total (31.112) stays almost exact to tpdev_pbp (31.1), so keep the runtime lineup and annotate the residual as an upstream scorer/chronology error.

Derrick Coleman is credited with P2 block/rebound events before the NBA PBP finally records his sub at 2:39. BBR credits the earlier plays, and the live minute total is already essentially exact to official/tpdev, so the remaining residual is an upstream missing-substitution chronology error, not a local correction opportunity.

David Wingate's P2/P3 event rows are explicitly documented as source-attribution errors, and his live minute total (12.183) stays much closer to tpdev_pbp (12.517) and BBR (12.0) than to official (13.05). Treat the three event rows and linked 0.867 minute tail as source-limited upstream error.

P4 E402 is a Schrempf rebound credited before the source records his 5:33 entry sub. BBR and tpdev both place him on court by then, and the local one-event override only swaps the live row for a broken follow-on sub row. Keep the runtime lineup and annotate this as an upstream event-ordering conflict.

Late-quarter chronology/source split in P3 keeps this game in the true open blocker lane. The tested BOS starter and NYK window probes are contradictory rather than promotable, so classify the remaining rows as candidate_systematic_defect instead of source-limited.

This whole-game minute residual is better explained as an upstream source conflict than a remaining parser defect. The live output matches BBR minutes on 18 of 20 players, and the only two BBR misses are a single Vaughn/Hendrickson swap where tpdev_pbp stays much closer to the live output than to BBR. There are no event-on-court rows, no severe minute outliers, and the official box matches only 1 of 20 player-minute rows, so keep the raw residual visible but classify the game as source_limited_upstream_error rather than an open candidate_systematic_defect.

This whole-game minute and PM residual is better explained as an upstream source conflict than a remaining parser defect. The Jeffries after-the-buzzer 0:00 three is already dropped by the live row override, counting-stat audit is clean, there are no event-on-court rows, and there are no severe minute outliers. After that drop, the live output still matches pbpstats minutes on 23 of 23 players and BBR minutes on 22 of 23 players, while the only BBR miss is the Steven/Steve Smith join issue and the official/tpdev box minutes match 0 of 23 player rows. Keep the raw residual visible, but classify the game as source_limited_upstream_error rather than an open lineup blocker.

A.C. Green P4 E511 rebound is the only remaining open row for this game. The local event flip is real but fails activation because it worsens the minute profile, while the current live Green/Bradley minutes already match official, tpdev_pbp, and BBR almost exactly. Treat E511 as an upstream event-ordering conflict and keep the runtime lineup unchanged.

P3 E349 is the broken source substitution row 'Lauderdale FOR Garrett' even though Garrett was already removed earlier. The live Denver P3 windows already repair the downstream lineup and explicitly leave only this broken sub-out half unresolved. Annotate the event row as source-limited while keeping the game's remaining minute tradeoff open.

P2 E172 credits Kukoc with a defensive-goaltending violation at the exact 6:32 clock where the live lineup subs him out. The current P1 bootstrap state keeps the minute profile exact-to-near-exact, and the tested P2 local fixes clear the event only by worsening minutes. Keep the runtime lineup and treat this row as a same-clock upstream boundary conflict.

P2 E185 credits Armstrong with a foul at the exact 5:36 clock where the live lineup ends his stint. This is another same-clock sub-out boundary row in a game whose live minute profile is already effectively exact, and there is no validated local fix that improves the event row without harming minutes. Keep the runtime lineup and annotate the event as source-limited upstream boundary conflict.

P4 E376 credits Kerr with a foul at the exact 7:25 clock where the live lineup ends his stint. Like the Kukoc and Armstrong rows, this is a same-clock exit-boundary conflict in a game with essentially exact minutes and no validated safe local fix. Keep the runtime lineup and mark the event as source-limited upstream boundary conflict.

P2 E102 is the phantom 'Gary Trent FOR Kevin Garnett' row even though Minnesota's validated Q2 lineup already has Trent active and Garnett off. The remaining game profile is otherwise clean except the direct Garnett/Trent plus-minus swap, so keep the runtime lineup and annotate this impossible sub-out row as source-limited upstream boundary noise rather than a new local override target.

P2 E126 is the phantom 'Mbah a Moute FOR Thomas' row at the exact Q2 boundary. `period_starters_v6` and `full_pbp_new` agree that Milwaukee already starts Q2 with Mbah a Moute active and Kurt Thomas off, while cached pbpv3 still injects the contradictory 12:00 sub row. Keep the runtime lineup unchanged and mark this impossible period-start substitution row as source-limited upstream boundary noise; the remaining Q4 plus-minus residue is a separate same-clock boundary effect.
